title: Avionics Sub-System Repair and Overhaul
entry_mechanism: PSAP Track 1 — International avionics MRO specialist attracted to Alsalam facility with Saudi understudy programme
executive_summary:
Saudi Arabia's military aviation fleet operates some of the most sophisticated avionics systems in the world — AN/APG-63 and AN/APG-82 radars on the F-15SA, Euroradar CAPTOR-E on the Typhoon, and Rockwell Collins flight management systems across multiple platforms. Today, component-level avionics repair is performed almost entirely outside the Kingdom, with units shipped to US and European MRO facilities under OEM service agreements. Node MA-6 targets the establishment of in-Kingdom avionics component repair and overhaul (CRO) capability — moving from line maintenance (replacing LRUs) to shop-level repair (repairing LRUs to serviceable standard).

The global avionics MRO market was valued at $7.8 billion in 2024, growing at 4.2% CAGR. Military avionics CRO is a high-margin, high-skill segment requiring specialist test equipment, OEM-licensed repair procedures, and security clearances for classified systems. Saudi Arabia's avionics CRO dependency represents both a strategic vulnerability and a significant annual import cost estimated at $180–250 million.

Phase II timing is correct: avionics CRO requires the quality system infrastructure (AS9100, EASA Part 145) established in Phase I at Alsalam, and the Saudi avionics technician pipeline initiated in Phase I. Without that foundation, a Phase I avionics CRO facility would lack the workforce and regulatory baseline to achieve OEM qualification.

companies.name: Elbit Systems
companies.rationale: Elbit has significant avionics CRO capability and Middle East relationships. Note: engagement subject to Saudi-Israeli normalisation progress. Alternative: pursue via a European JV partner.

companies.name: Collins Aerospace
companies.rationale: Collins supplies avionics to F-15SA and has Saudi Arabia MRO obligations under existing contracts. Licensed CRO at Alsalam aligns with Collins' authorised repair network strategy.
companies.contact: Collins Aerospace VP Military Aftermarket, Middle East

companies.name: Thales
companies.rationale: Thales supplies avionics to Typhoon and has ITAR-free European alternatives for many CRO capabilities. Thales has established Saudi Arabia presence through Airbus Defence programmes.
companies.contact: Thales VP Defence Avionics, Gulf Region

companies.name: Heico Corporation
companies.rationale: Heico is the world's largest independent avionics repair company with OEM-equivalent repair capabilities and a cost-competitive model suited to a new Saudi CRO operation.
companies.contact: Heico Aviation Group VP International Business

companies.name: Lufthansa Technik
companies.rationale: LHT has avionics CRO capabilities across military and civil platforms and a proven JV establishment model. Their existing Saudia Technic relationship provides a Saudi entry point.

partners.name: Alsalam Aircraft Company
partners.current: Line-level avionics maintenance; LRU removal and replacement on F-15 and Typhoon
partners.upgrade: Establish dedicated avionics CRO shop within Alsalam; Collins Aerospace licensing for F-15 avionics categories; EASA Part 145 extension; Saudi avionics technician apprenticeship (3-year programme)

partners.name: SAMI Electronics
partners.current: Defense electronics assembly and test; some communications equipment maintenance
partners.upgrade: Extend to avionics board-level repair; invest in automated test equipment (ATE); obtain IPC-A-610 Class 3 and AS9100 qualification for avionics CRO scope
partners.timeline: 24 months to first avionics CRO delivery

partners.name: King Abdulaziz City for Science and Technology (KACST)
partners.current: Electronics research, signal processing, some defence electronics testing capability
partners.upgrade: KACST avionics test laboratory co-funded by GADD; develop Saudi test procedures for non-ITAR avionics categories; build Saudi test engineers pipeline

partners.name: KFUPM Electrical Engineering
partners.current: Electronics engineering graduates; some embedded systems and signal processing specialisation
partners.upgrade: GADD-KFUPM avionics technician programme; 2-year avionics CRO immersion at Alsalam; GACA Aircraft Maintenance Engineer (Avionics) licence pathway
partners.timeline: 24 months to first qualified Saudi avionics technicians
